item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Idea: the reason that a chemistry teacher's the only one immune to the effect of the water tower is that she's the only one aware of the the threat posed by Di-Hydrogen Monoxide.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was great. A predatory watertower is a hell of a concept.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The fact that they say "with familiarity" and don't simply isolate monitoring staff from directly observing or making contact with 2646, implies that it isn't limited to visual contact or proximity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2646-1 also imposes a cognitohazardous effect on all people with familiarity with the object2, such that they believe its current location to be where the object has always been, regardless of its unusual placement.

I think that "with familiarity" is a little too broad, since it only seems to affect people who've seen it and/or have been near it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I always did feel like water towers were odd structures. I'm staring out the window at the office here and there's one just over the tree line, trying to look all friendly, fluted like a Greek column and sporting a cheery mural painted on the top…but it can't fool me. I'm watching it like a hawk.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You should take a picture of it and post it here! Creepy-looking structures are always good to have on hand for reference.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this skip, so much so that I felt the need to make edits to fix a number of grammatical problems. Mea culpa if I accidentally altered the meaning of anything in the process, though I don't believe I did.

Nicely done, C&S! +1 for sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't let the bugger catch you looking at it or it'll have you. +1 ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is an actual picture of an actual water tower i took that's actually near an actual school. Heh. And it is very jarring. That's probably why this idea came to me while i was staring at it suspiciously while hurrying to my car.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this article, I kinda feel thirsty reading it :P

I'm upvoting because it's excellent content, but I'm not too sure about the picture. The tower kinda looks too fake and it's quite jarring.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: God fucking damn, CAS. This is real good creepy shit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Those bodies had always been there inside the tower."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Agents were dispatched to inform families of the deceased that they had perished in the reported gas leak. However, families in question were discovered to be under the cognitohazardous effect of SCP-2646 and had no memory of the individuals killed in the incident.

They did not recognize the bodies in the water.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's this horrible water tower near my brother's college. The damn thing just looks haunted. Despite the provided picture, that's the water tower I'm imagining when I read this skip.

+1. Take your upvote, and watch your back for water towers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the typo catch. One always slips by no matter what.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is purely great. Tells a story, lets on more than it shows, keeps the unnerving angle growing and growing, all-around a fantastic skip. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've always thought water towers were a little spooky and sinister-looking, so this scip had me from the start. Well-written and all-around interesting. You have my upvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's more to it than just a moving water tower. Note what it did/caused people to do to the school building.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Students and were administered Class B

Students and ???

Other than that, I enjoyed it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait, the stated effect is that it's a moving watertower in which people beleive it's always been where it currently is. But how did that make the families of the drowned people forget them?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, a water tower that demands recognition is a lovely concept. I also love the execution. So I guess I just love this. I wonder why that woman is not affected though… That seems extremely odd, and intriguing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Doesn't seem necessary. It's just another step the reader has to go through to access content.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Lovely. I don't mind a good wall of text when they're this well done. You may want to put the incident reports under collapsibles though. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Creepy. Would have been creepier if I was thirsty at the moment, but that's obviously not under your control. Solid +1 from me regardless.

item.title: SCP-2646
item.content:encoded: Thanks for reading! Looking forward to everyone's input!
